description: Acturus is an austere Hjorden system where survival culture and martial pride blend into a relentless industrial rhythm: extract, refine, forge, and fuel. The Hjorden here favour durable, repairable hardware There are rogue traders here so be ready to enforce your side of agreements.

Al Mare system is the priciple and home of the USFP Member race the Al’Marey. The Al'Marey have a few scatered systems in USFP space and live semi nomadicly.

The system’s most infamous feature is the shattered remains of Hugo, a former super-Earth-class world destroyed in a cataclysmic event approximately 8,244 years ago. The dense debris field left behind poses navigational hazards but also serves as a rich source of salvage and rare minerals it also has several spectacular nebular.

ntara is the Hjorden’s pragmatic answer to “soft power”: a hard-world provisioning system that produces compact rations, rugged clothing, and trauma kits designed for raiders, miners, and frontier militias. Hjorden caravans broker these supplies far beyond Antara, building influence with practical necessities rather than threats. It’s still Hjorden society—status is earned, not given—but Antara’s merchants are canny enough to win with generosity when brutality would be expensive.

Ashia is a Major Fleet base for the TSN and Boarder system
Colonised in the earlt 2100's by the Kerstal - a long lived and slow to reproduce race Ashia has become a regional capital for the Upper Arc. The Ashia Fleet base is one of the 2nd Fleets largest and has some limited drydock facilites.

Athorn is an Al’marey-pocket society built around modular living—habitats, factories, even governance are snapped together like standardized blocks. That mindset makes Athorn excellent at producing consistent industrial goods and reliable warp subassemblies in small runs, ideal for fleets that can’t afford long downtime. Visitors find Athorn’s “polite distance” unsettling: they’re cooperative, but everything—access, labour, even hospitality—has a published spec and a price.

Atilia is a pale-yellow K-class star with a wide, dusty inner belt nicknamed “The Chalkline” because it looks like a faint on most civilian scopes. Relitivly Poor System that has taken soem serious damage in the recent wars. The Jump From Atila to Helios is One way so passing trade is limited.
Atlantis is the Regional Capital for the Lower Arc

Black Spire is an unusual Sytem that has 3 Black holes. Other then that the system bearly quilifes as an outpost, some mineing. The Ashes explorioty Gate has recently come online provideing a basis for the primary exploration of Vesper Point.

Brightfall has 4 seperate govenments each consiting of two Civil class stations and an industrial facility. unforunetlyt the govenments do not get along with each other and disputes are frequent between them. The single jump point means that a lot of trade simply bypasses the system. Piracy is rife in Brightfall due to the conflicted nature of the area. blockades of the jump point are not unheard of.

A lush, water-rich system discovered late in the Confederation’s expansion, Chawaras is both a breadbasket and a biotech frontier. Vast floating farms and coral-based biohabitats thrive in the upper layers of Chawaras IV’s endless oceans. The planet’s lower depths, however, are dangerous — bioluminescent predators known as shale serpents prowl the abyss, occasionally breaching into shallow colonies. The Ximni Genetic Council operates here, experimenting with ways to reverse radiation-linked mutations from their ancient apocalypse. Despite its beauty, the system is infamous for “Song Plagues” — genetically engineered algae that produce hypnotic harmonics capable of driving listeners mad.

Corus is home to the planet Corus and has a number of obiting stations. notably it is the HQ system for the Corus Cargo Co. Fort Grey is a TSN Marine training facility. Corus is an abundant farming world and is noted for haveing an extensive railway network.

Danae is one of the newer colonies in the USFP, only just achiving Colony Status in XXXX. Its econmoy is mainly focused on Agriculture however the Long Bar Exploritory Gate is present here takeing advantage of strong jump point that allows access to the Euphine Expanse and beyond.

Etrei is a Kralien system where knowledge is a weapon and secrets are currency; its institutions are built around surveillance theory, signal manipulation, and the quiet art of misdirection. The Kralien here export AI cores tuned for pattern recognition and quantum components used in high-end comms and sensor suites. Outsiders can trade here—if they accept that every conversation may be recorded for “academic purposes.”
system under the League Of Traders Democray

Eyukua is a warm, garden-heavy system settled predominantly by the Veldari—stoic, long-lived agronomists who build communities around soil stewardship and conservative bio-engineering. Their exports are practical: resilient crops, clean organics, and medical packs designed for remote systems where a broken bone can become a death sentence. Eyukua dislikes “heroics”; they prefer prevention, redundancy, and quiet competence—until someone threatens their seed vaults.
System discovered in 2275. Principally a Research system with light industry and a rear storage armory built during the first spiral arm war.

Fymla is the technological nerve center of the Confederation, home to sprawling orbital dockyards and the Ximni Gateforge, where the latest generation of jump coils and gate relays are constructed. The system’s innermost planet, Fym-II, is tidally locked — its permanent twilight band houses vast energy farms that harvest the planet’s magnetic storms. Fymla’s shipyards are constantly alive with neon-lit scaffolds, and the local culture celebrates engineers as celebrities, often featuring them in holo-dramas and competitions. Fymla is also notorious for “slipfire storms” — spontaneous bursts of electromagnetic radiation that can scramble unshielded navigation systems.
Autogenerated terrain (small, 300000 radius) on 2026-01-24.
System under the League Of Traders Federation

Helios has an infamous history. In 2240 it was the center ofa hegemony superweapon, The Planet was deemed a class 6 Bio Hazard and is uninhabited to this day, the uncounted millions who perished are memorialized in an orbital station, with a large habitation complex holding many of the former citizens. As a boarder system it sees moderate traffic and is a hive of smuggling though has little piracy.
This is a well established system a joint venture by Terra and the AlMare Union. Contains a significant Shipyard complex and an Armory point though lacks in TSN Facilitys otherwise.

Serving as the political and logistical heart of the Ximni Confederation, Isscar became the de facto capital when the population of Renaera could no longer sustain open governance. The temperate world of Issen Prime hosts the Confederation Forum — an immense orbital complex where votes are transmitted in real time from across the Confederation. Isscar’s orbital lanes are crowded with freighters and jump couriers, and the system’s twin suns make navigation challenging, leading to frequent “shadow drift” incidents when ships miscalculate their approach vectors. The Isscar Jump Nexus is a key Jump Point.

Jotar is a Torgoth-controlled system run in what even torgoths view as a distopian way: mine bosses, enforcers, and debt-bound labour all feeding a steady output of ore, crude industrial stock, and fuel. The Torgoth here value utility and dominance; negotiations are direct and often backed by demonstrations of strength. For outsiders, Jotar is profitable and dangerous in equal measure—an excellent place to buy bulk goods if you can survive the terms.

Khoros is a harsh, mineral-rich system dominated by the Khorassi—adapted to high-radiation industrial life and fiercely protective of what they consider ancestral claim-zones in the belt. Their wealth comes from Denebite strikes and the nano-treated alloys derived from it, shipped out in small, guarded lots. Khoros doesn’t trust outsiders with maps or methods; deals are done face-to-face, and betrayal is repaid across generations.

Kratos is a hard-edged, pragmatic system with a red-brown super-Earth (Kratos III) that never quite terraformed right. As such its considered a dust bowl of a system, there rich asteroids here but explotation has yet to be pushed and only rudimentary surveying has happend.

A moderatly developed system Krisenda suffers from beeing near the regional capital of Atlantis. That said its a nicde shorcut to the Long Bar Region and is less monitored by autoritys then Atlantis. Smuggling thorough this system is rife and the odd pirate is to be found here.
Kudos is a newly claimed system that was recently discovered after the Freya Fracture Inident in 2245

Meera is an Arvonian system where biology and aesthetics intertwine: medical exports are world-class, but so are the “cosmetic organics” and adaptive biomaterials that make Arvonian commerce feel like diplomacy with a price tag. The society is graceful, matriarchal, and intensely social; reputation matters as much as law, and bargains are sealed with favours as often as with credits. Visitors leave healthier… and often indebted in ways they don’t fully understand.

The Memoras Free Zone is the Confederation’s great bazaar — a chaotic frontier world where trade, innovation, and revelry blur together. Located on the edge of Ximni-controlled space, Memoras orbits a binary star that floods the skies in constant aurorae. The world’s capital, Doxx City, is infamous for its floating taverns, neon airships, and open-air trade domes. Merchants from across the USFP and even the Hegemony conduct business here, protected by local neutrality laws. Memoras is also home to the Circle of Xim, a semi-religious order of storytellers and philosophers who record and judge acts of courage across the Confederation.

Oberon is a quiet system one step up from a waypoint system, disptie beeing on a major shiping route its a back water due to the fact its single jump point acts as both incoming and out going. its rare for traders to spend more time here then it takes to jump in, update there nav files and jump out.

There is however some mineing, industry and a small Armory.
Reema is a border-economy system: a place where USFP military culture meets League merchant pragmatism. It’s not glamorous, but it’s where misunderstandings become incidents—fast.

The cradle of Ximni civilization, Renaera still bears the scars of the nuclear holocaust that nearly ended their species a millennium ago. Vast regions of the surface remain lifeless and glassed, their twisted metal skeletons now monuments to the “Folly of the First Age.” Enormous biodome cities and underground arcologies dot the habitable belts, powered by geothermal fusion reactors buried deep beneath the irradiated crust. The surviving ruins of Old Zenth, the pre-war capital, are preserved as a cultural relic and a place of pilgrimage. Orbiting Renaera is the Echo Ring, an artificial habitat containing the Confederation’s largest historical archives.

is a Kralien bio-industrial system that treats medicine as both healing and leverage: they excel at organics, nano-assisted delivery systems, and rapid-response clinics, but their research culture is comfortable walking right up to ethical lines. Trade is lucrative and unsettling—Setmari can save worlds, and they know it. For TSN, Setmari is a potential ally and a potential nightmare, depending on who holds the contracts.

Sierra. This system is a flashpoint whilst there are some Kralien bases towards the Elex Jump Point its largely uninhabited and a buffer system between the USFP and Hegemony. There are a number of jump points in this system that have not been stabilised or explored properly by the USFP. Its also a system that the factions of the hegemony are offten seen transiting to get to each others space.
Sumri is a system under the League Of Traders Democray
Autogenerated TestSystem. Generated 2026-01-24. Stations: 25. Platforms: 9. Statics: 17. Terrain: 4.

Tibur is a welcoming, brochure-perfect crossroads built around Planet Tibur, an easygoing Earthlike world with broad blue seas and clean continental sprawl. A soft violet nebula band paints the mid-lanes with pearlescent light, while a long asteroid run provides guided scenic routes, zero-g sport-tows, and 'hands-on' prospecting tours. Signature stops include the glittering Astral Agora market promenade and the indulgent Interstellar Spas—where even layovers feel like holidays.

Tilminra is a Terran-settled “breadbasket with teeth”: sprawling agri-habitats and orbital processing plants feed nearby routes while a strong civic militia protects the supply chain. The system’s secondary strength is frontier pharmacology—bio-engineered organics and practical med supplies meant for hard living and long travel. Tilminra’s culture is proud and communal, but strained: everyone knows a single serious disruption could turn abundance into panic.

Titan is a USFP industrial anchor where Terran heavy fabrication supports fleet maintenance, station construction, and the endless demand for standardized parts. It’s not glamorous—Titan’s pride is reliability—yet its yards and warehouses quietly influence regional power because whoever controls Titan’s output controls readiness. Civilians live in the shadow of production quotas and dock schedules, and the TSN presence is constant, both as customer and as guarantor of stability.

Perched near the edge of known Hegemony space, Tjorath is the Ximni Confederation’s shield. Its primary planet, Tjorath IV, is a storm-wracked world rich in volatile gases mined for starship fuel. Orbital installations double as military academies and test sites for new Jump Coil prototypes, some of which have resulted in “translocation incidents” — ships appearing light-years off course or even in other timelines, according to persistent rumors. Tjorath’s citizens are proud of their role as defenders and often sport clan crests and flight badges as symbols of honor. The system is under constant patrol by the Confederation Vanguard Fleets.
Toray is a cold, dim system with long orbital periods and plenty of empty. Notable for its Comercial Damage control and Navigation Facility, otherwise unremarkable.

A Stage 3 Colony of the USFP, Mixed population including Grezintian, Humans, Aquarins and others. Self sufficiant with minor reaserch, contains a major starship scraping facility - Sams Junkyard. No notable Military
Vesper Point is a recently opened system undergoing a primary survey. the USFP have made claim to this system.
Vilnara An estabished Industrial system with some key engineering and Shipyard facilitys Including an extensive fleet base
Volantis is a frount line system on the border of the Hegemony, even in peace time its offten host to Diplomatic and tradeing missions - Check your fire!
Waypoint System - Standard Search and Rescue station, othwise uninhabited
Waypoint System - Standard Search and Rescue station, othwise uninhabited

52 is a moderatly well traveld system - as such theres offten a patrol beeing sent through. that said it has a single Search and Rescue Station, and an inderpendent Mining Operation and thats about it.

Waypoint system Standard S & R outpost pluse a minor comertical venture. as such the uninhabited Development status of the Waypoint 60 is streaching a point. rather to say that there is no recognised USFP member claim on the system.
Waypoint System - uninhabited Single Search and Rescue station, Considered a hardship posting. Lightly traveled High Pirate Activity
